title: Glean Entity Graph
description: The normalised, provenance-tracked output of a Glean scan. A single container holding deduplicated entities and the relationships between them. Every entity carries first-class provenance. Version 0.1.0 (v1 infra/domain scope).

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the document conforms to.

entities.description: Deduplicated entities. One record per real-world entity, even if multiple tools reported it.

edges.description: Directed relationships between entities, keyed by entity id.

target.description: The seed target of the scan (a root domain in v1).

authorisation.description: Free-text note recording the basis on which this target was authorised for scanning.

tools_run.description: Which adapters/tools contributed to this scan, and how.

raw_output_ref.description: Path/URI to the archived raw output for this tool run (reproducibility + provenance).

method.description: Whether the data was collected passively (no packets to target) or actively (touches the target). Enforced separation per charter.

entityType.description: The v1 infra/domain entity vocabulary. Extending this list is a schema version bump.

provenanceEntry.description: One assertion, by one tool, that this entity exists. An entity seen by N tools carries N provenance entries. This array is the substrate of the provenance-retention metric.

source_module.description: Optional sub-source within a tool (e.g. a specific theHarvester source, an Amass data source).

raw_record_ref.description: Pointer into the archived raw output (e.g. a line number, JSON path, or record id) so any surfaced claim is traceable to source bytes.

confidence.description: Optional tool-reported or adapter-assigned confidence. Absent means unspecified, not zero.

id.description: Stable deterministic identity key. Convention: '<type>:<canonical_value>'. Two tools reporting the same real-world entity MUST produce the same id after canonicalisation, so dedup is a key match.

value.description: The canonical, normalised representation (lowercased host, compressed IPv6, lowercased email, etc.). Canonicalisation rules are defined in ADR-0001.

attributes.description: Type-specific fields. Kept open so each entity type can carry its own detail without a schema bump; documented per-type in ADR-0001.

provenance.description: At least one entry. Non-empty provenance is invariant: an entity with no source cannot exist in a Glean graph.

priority.description: Deterministic prioritisation output. Computed in code (ADR-0004), never by the LLM. Optional until the scoring stage runs.

score.description: Deterministic priority score from the ADR-0004 rubric.

rank.description: 1-based position in the scan-wide priority ordering.

signals.description: The named signals that contributed to the score (e.g. 'exposed_service', 'breach_hit'), for explainability and eval.

edge.description: Directed relationship. source_id and target_id MUST reference entity ids present in the graph.

relation.description: Relationship vocabulary for v1 infra scope.

provenance.description: Optional provenance for the relationship itself (which tool asserted this edge).
